This is unlike the defer object, however, which places high priority messages at the front of the low priority queue, and passes low priority messages immediately. The deferlow object is useful to preserve message sequencing that might otherwise be reversed with the defer object and/or guarantee that an incoming message will be deferred to a future servicing of the low priority queue even if that message is low priority itself.

delay.name: delay
delay.description: Holds a bang for a specified amount of time before sending it to the next object. This object uses the Max time format syntax, so the delay time (which is normally specified in milliseconds) can also be set to other fixed or tempo-relative values.

detonate.name: detonate
detonate.digest: Play a score of note events
detonate.description: Provides score playback managed using Max messages. The score may be loaded from a MIDI file, or generated using Max functions. The score is not limited to MIDI notes and values; any information can be stored and played back with detonate.

inlets.digest: Control Messages, Time Parameter When Recording

dial.name: dial
dial.digest: Output numbers using an onscreen dial
dial.description: Outputs numbers according to its degree of rotation. dial can be set with a certain range, offset, multiplier, as well as numerous visual settings.

dialog.name: dialog
dialog.description: Displays a dialog box with a selection of appearance modes. In Default mode, the dialog object permits entry of a symbol (as text) and sends it out the outlet when you click on the "OK" button in the dialog box. In the other modes, the dialog object displays text, but doesn't permit data entry.

inlets.digest: 0 Sets Output as Symbol, 1 as List/Message

outlets.digest: bang When User Clicks No in Extended mode

dict.name: dict
dict.description: Use the dict object to create named dictionaries, clone existing dictionaries, and query existing dictionaries to access their data.

inlets.digest: dictionary input clones and sends, bang sends
dictionary input clones but does not send

outlets.digest: value for an invidual key in response to the 'get' message
list of keys in response to 'getkeys' message
list of dictionaries in response to 'getnames' message

arguments.name: name
arguments.digest: Name to be associated with this dictionary

arguments.name: filename
arguments.digest: Name of a JSON or YAML file to be imported

dict.codebox.name: dict.codebox
dict.codebox.description: The dict.codebox object is a UI object for display and editing of dictionaries. Use the dict.codebox object to create named dictionaries, clone existing dictionaries, and query existing dictionaries to access their data.

inlets.digest: dictionary input clones and sends, bang sends
dictionary input clones but does not send

outlets.digest: value for an invidual key in response to the 'get' message
list of keys in response to 'getkeys' message
list of dictionaries in response to 'getnames' message

dictionary to compare with dictionary at left inlet

dict.deserialize.name: dict.deserialize
dict.deserialize.description: Use the dict.deserialize object to create a dictionary from text passed in using Max's dictionary syntax or JSON. Alternatively, compressed forms of either format may be used.

inlets.digest: dictionary whose contents will be deserialized

arguments.name: name
arguments.digest: Name of the dictionary to create from serialized input

dict.group.name: dict.group
dict.group.description: Use the dict.group object to build up a dictionary by sending key-value pairs as lists. The key-value pairs will be collected into the dictionary until a 'bang' is received. The 'bang' will send out the dictionary and start the process over again.

inlets.digest: dictionary syntax for appending a key/value pair, bang sends the dictionary and resets

arguments.name: name
arguments.digest: Name of the dictionary to create

dict.iter.name: dict.iter
dict.iter.digest: Stream the content of a dictionary
dict.iter.description: Use the dict.iter object to send all key-value pairs out from a dictionary as a series of standard Max list messages.

dict.join.name: dict.join
dict.join.digest: Merge the content of two dictionaries
dict.join.description: Use the dict.join object to merge the content of two dictionaries together into a single dictionary.

inlets.digest: dictionary to combined with dictionary at right inlet
dictionary to combined with dictionary at left inlet

outlets.digest: dictionary of entries combined from both inlets

arguments.name: default-values
arguments.digest: Dictionary syntax composing a dictionary to be joined with the object's input

dict.pack.digest: Create a dictionary and set its values
dict.pack.description: Use the dict.pack object to create a dictionary and set its values using dedicated inlets.

arguments.name: name

arguments.name: default-values
arguments.digest: Dictionary syntax specifying initial keys and values composing the dictionary

dict.print.name: dict.print
dict.print.digest: Post a dictionary to the Max Console
dict.print.description: Use the dict.print object to post the content of a dictionary to the Max Console. For more control over how the printing is formatted, use dict.iter and print.

dict.route.name: dict.route
dict.route.description: Use the dict.route object to compare two dictionaries. If the dictionary received at the left inlet meets the specifications set by the dictionary at the right inlet, then pass the dictionary through the left outlet. Otherwise passes the dictionary through the right outlet.

arguments.name: default-values
arguments.digest: Dictionary syntax composing a dictionary to be compared with the object's input

dict.serialize.name: dict.serialize
dict.serialize.digest: Convert a dictionary's content to text
dict.serialize.description: Use the dict.serialize object to output a serialized form of the contents of dictionary in text format. The text may be Dictionary Syntax, JSON, or Base64-compressed forms of these formats.

inlets.digest: dictionary whose contents will be serialized

dict.slice.name: dict.slice
dict.slice.digest: Split a dictionary into two dictionaries
dict.slice.description: Use the dict.slice object to split a dictionary into two dictionaries. The first dictionary will be created from a set of keys you provide, sent to the left outlet. The remaining dictionary content will form the second dictionary, sent to the right outlet.

outlets.digest: dictionary output sliced of specified keys
series of key/value pairs sliced from the dictionary

dict.strip.name: dict.strip
dict.strip.description: Use the dict.strip object to remove the specified keys from a dictionary and return their values through an outlet. Following this operation, the dictionary will no longer contain the specified keys.

outlets.digest: dictionary output stripped of specified keys
series of key/value pairs stripped from the dictionary

arguments.name: keys
arguments.digest: Keys to be stripped from the dictionary

dict.unpack.name: dict.unpack
dict.unpack.description: Use the dict.unpack object to return the values of specified keys through dedicated outlets.

arguments.name: name
arguments.digest: Name of the dictionary to unpack

arguments.name: default-values
arguments.digest: Dictionary syntax specifying initial keys and values to be fetched

dict.view.name: dict.view
dict.view.digest: View the contents of a dictionary
dict.view.description: Use the dict.view object to view the contents of a dictionary.

dropfile.name: dropfile
dropfile.description: dropfile defines a region for dragging and dropping files into and then outputs the filepath and filetype upon file input.

drunk.name: drunk

drunk.description: Performs a "drunken" walk by outputting random numbers within a specified step range.

inlets.digest: bang Outputs Random Step, int Sets Value

arguments.name: maximum value
arguments.digest: Set the maximum number for output

dsp.name: dsp
dsp.description: This is where you send the messages

error.name: error
error.description: Listens for and reports Max errors as message output. This will allow for error management in cases where it is not appropriate to display the Max window.

inlets.digest: 1 Turns Error Listening On, 0 Turns It Off

expr.name: expr
expr.description: Evaluate an expression using a C-like language. Variables and operators are used to create output values.

filedate.name: filedate
filedate.digest: Report the modification date of a file

filein.name: filein
filein.digest: Read and access a file of binary data
filein.description: filein reads a file of binary data and outputs the data at various points in the file given the appropriate input.

filepath.name: filepath
filepath.digest: Manage and report on the Max search path
filepath.description: Provides access to the Max search path, and allows modification to the search path used by a patch.

inlets.digest: set Changes Path, append Adds Path

filewatch.name: filewatch
filewatch.description: Watch a specific file and reports a bang whenever that file is altered.

float.name: float
float.description: float can store and output any given floating-point number.

inlets.digest: bang Outputs Value, int Sets and Outputs Value

flonum.name: flonum
flonum.description: Display, input, and output floating-point numbers in a number box.

inlets.digest: Set Displayed Number and Repeat to Output

flush.name: flush
flush.digest: Output MIDI note-offs for held notes
flush.description: Outputss note-off messages for any held note-ons. flush keeps track of all note-ons passed through it, and produces note-off messages for any held notes when it receives a bang message.

inlets.digest: Pitch Input, bang Flushes Held Notes

folder.name: folder

folder.description: Outputs all of the file names in a given folder. This can be especially useful for loading a umenu.

follow.name: follow

follow.description: follow records pitches, or you can give it a MIDI file, in which case it looks at the file's note-ons and ignores other events. When it is "following" it outputs the index of the last note matched.

inlets.digest: MIDI Pitch Input, Many Other Messages

fontlist.name: fontlist
fontlist.description: Outputs a list of system fonts and, optionally, their system identification numbers. Optionally filters the list by font-family.

forward.name: forward
forward.digest: Send messages to specified receive objects
forward.description: Relays messages to other objects remotely. Unlike the send object, the destination receive object of forward can be changed with each message.

inlets.digest: Message To Remote, send Changes Receiver
read Changes File, offset Changes Visible Area

freebang.digest: Send a bang when a patcher is freed

fromsymbol.name: fromsymbol
fromsymbol.description: fromsymbol will take the individual characters in a symbol and convert them from a symbol back to numbers/messages.

ftom.description: ftom converts frequency to MIDI note numbers

Sets Stored Element Value for Insertion

outlets.digest: bang Output When next Message Reaches End

function.name: function
function.description: Draw or store a set of x, y points as floating-point numbers. The output the entire function is useful as an input for line~. You can also get an interpolated y value for any x value.

inlets.digest: bang Outputs All, float Outputs Y at X, list Edits

outlets.digest: Interpolated Y (float) for Input X Interpolated Y (float) for Input X

funnel.name: funnel
funnel.digest: Tag data with its inlet number
funnel.description: Identifies the inlet of incoming data. It can be used to store values into a table or coll based on their source, or used to set a destination with an object such as spray.

io_rule.description: First arg sets inlet count. Outlets = 1. Default: 2 inlets.

gamepad.name: gamepad
gamepad.description: Tracks and outputs the button, joystick, trigger, and sensor events from all connected gamepad controllers. Send a device control messages for haptic feedback rumble events and device led color with the senddevice message. The gamepad object is a lightweight wrapper for the Simple DirectMedia Layer 2.0 Library's gamepad implementation.

inlets.digest: 0 Closes gate, Non-zero Opens Gate Outlet

io_rule.description: First arg sets outlet count. Inlets = 2 (control + input). Default: 1 outlet.

getattr.name: getattr
getattr.description: Provides a user interface to query attribute values from an object. You can also retrieve a list of all available attributes for the attached object.

ggate.name: ggate
ggate.digest: Send input to one of two outlets
ggate.description: Switches the right inlet between two output pathways.

grab.name: grab
grab.digest: Intercept the output of another object
grab.description:
grab can send a message and extract the result from the receiving object.
		
		Note: The grab object cannot be used to communicate from a send to a receive between Max for Live devices.

outlets.digest: Connect to Object That Will Receive Message

hi.name: hi
hi.description: Provides input from human interface peripherals (i.e. Trackpad, Keyboard, and others).

outlets.digest: (message) device enumeration output in menu format

hid.name: hid

outlets.digest: hid report element info and value
device enumeration output in menu format

hint.name: hint
hint.description: When you mouse over a hint, you'll see a message appear on the screen below the area defined by the hint. The hint object has a number of messages you can use to change its appearance.

histo.name: histo
histo.digest: Create a histogram of numbers received
histo.description: Records and outputs histogram data of the numbers it receives.

inlets.digest: Numbers to Keep Track Of (and 'clear' Message)

hover.name: hover
hover.description: Sends out the scripting names of any object over which the cursor is hovering.

outlets.digest: Reports the scripting name of the object over which the mouse is hovering at any given time.
Scripting Name of Object Mouse Has Just Left
none if Object Mouse Has Just Left Has No Scripting Name

if.name: if
if.description: Evaluates input according to a conditional statement specified in an if-then-else form.

imovie.name: imovie
imovie.description: Plays a movie in a user-interface object within the patcher window.

inlets.digest: int Locates, start Plays, Many Other Commands

outlets.digest: Movie Time in Response To time Message
Horiz Mouse Location When Clicked in Movie
Vert Mouse Location When Clicked in Movie

incdec.name: incdec
incdec.description: Increment or Decrement a value. When connected to a number box, Click the upper half of the object to increment, click the lower half to decrement.

inlet.name: inlet
inlet.digest: Receive messages from outside a patcher
inlet.description: Receive messages from outside the patcher wherever it is embedded. Each inlet object in a patcher will show up as an inlet at the top of an object box when the patch is used inside another patcher (as an object or a subpatch). Messages sent into such an inlet will be received by the inlet object in the subpatch.

int.description: int can store and output any given integer number.

iter.digest: Break a list into individual messages
iter.description: Unpacks and outputs list contents one element at a time.

itoa.description: Convert a stream or list of up to 256 integer character codes into a symbol.

inlets.digest: Integer list converted to symbol, bang outputs current symbol
Integer list converted and concatenated to current symbol
Integer list converted and sets contents of symbol

jbox.name: jbox
jbox.description: Below is a list of attributes shared by all objects. If you want to change one of these attributes for an object based on the object box, you need to place the word sendbox in front of the attribute name, or use the object's Inspector.

jit.cellblock.name: jit.cellblock
jit.cellblock.digest: Edit rows and columns of data
jit.cellblock.description: Provides storage, viewing and editing of two-dimensional data. The format is similar to the "grid" display tools found in many other development environments. The current cell location, format, display and contents within jit.cellblock can be set with the mouse or by using Max messages.

jit_kernel.name: jit_kernel
jit_kernel.description: This is where you send those messages.

join.name: join
join.description: Takes separate untyped items and combines them into an output list.

io_rule.description: Number of arguments determines number of inlets. Default: 2 inlets.

js.name: js
js.description: Exposes the Javascript language (ECMAScript 5) and some Max specific extensions. The js object can be instantiated with a javascript filename or with numerical arguments to specify the number of outlets and inlets respectively. The default number of outlets and inlets are both 1.

jstrigger.name: jstrigger
jstrigger.description: The jstrigger object is similar to the trigger object, except that typed-in arguments within parentheses are passed to the Javascript evaluator. For more information on the Max implementation of Javascript, refer to the Javascript in Max manual. For complete information about Javascript itself, consult a reference book such as Javascript: The Definitive Guide by David Flanagan, published by O'Reilly.

jsui.name: jsui
jsui.digest: Javascript user interfaces and graphics (Legacy Engine)
jsui.description: Provides an environment to make user interface elements using Javascript (ECMAScript 5). This provides all of the programming tools available in the js object, but also exposes the mgraphics and sketch drawing routines for visual output.

jweb.name: jweb
jweb.description: The jweb object uses the cross-platform Chromium Embedded Framework (CEF) to host web pages within a Max UI object.

jweb~.name: jweb~
jweb~.description: The jweb~ object uses the cross-platform Chromium Embedded Framework (CEF) to host web pages within a Max UI object. Audio signals generated by the page are sent out the object's outlets.

key.name: key
key.description: Tracks and outputs the key-codes, ASCII values, and modifier-key values of key presses on the computer keyboard.

outlets.digest: Platform-Specific Keyboard Code of Key Pressed
Platform-Independent Keyboard Code of Key Pressed

keyup.name: keyup
keyup.description: Tracks and outputs the key-codes, ASCII values, and modifier-key values of key releases on the computer keyboard.

outlets.digest: Platform-Specific Keyboard Code of Key Released
Platform-Independent Keyboard Code of Key Released

kslider.name: kslider
kslider.digest: Output numbers from an onscreen keyboard
kslider.description: Outputs and displays note and velocity information using an on-screen keyboard.

outlets.digest: Outputs Key Value Changed or Received

inlets.digest: int Sets LED, bang Reverses It

outlets.digest: Outputs 1 or 0 When LED is Changed or Set

line.name: line
line.description: Generate ramps and line segments from one value to another within a specified amount of time.

linedrive.name: linedrive
linedrive.description: Scales number from one range to another with an exponential curve. Both the input and output ranges are expressed as single arguments representing the maximum value. The minimum values are the negative values of the ranges (argument * -1).

listbox.name: listbox
listbox.digest: Display and output numbers, lists, and messages
listbox.description: The number object displays and outputs either integers, floats, lists of numbers, or any message, depending on its format. flonum is a synonym for number in Float format, listbox is a synonym for number in List format, and textbox is a synonym for number in Text format.

inlets.digest: Set Displayed Input and Repeat to Output

listfunnel.name: listfunnel
listfunnel.description:
Outputs the elements of an incoming list in the format:
		
		[index] [element]
		
		for each element of the list.

inlets.digest: list in, Elements Will Be Output Preceded by Their Index

loadbang.name: loadbang
loadbang.digest: Send a bang when a patcher is loaded
loadbang.description: Outputs a bang automatically when the file is opened or when the patch is part of another file that is opened.

inlets.digest: Outputs bang When Patcher Window is Loaded

outlets.digest: Outputs bang When Patcher Window is Loaded

loadmess.name: loadmess
loadmess.digest: Send a message when a patch is loaded
loadmess.description: Outputs a message automatically when the file is opened, or when the patch is part of another file that is opened.

inlets.digest: Double-Click or bang to Re-Trigger Message

outlets.digest: Message Output When Patcher Is Loaded

makenote.name: makenote
makenote.description: Outputs a MIDI note-on message paired with a velocity value followed by a note-off message after a specified amount of time. This allows for generative MIDI output without having to manage note-off generation.

mappings.name: mappings
mappings.description: The mappings object allows you to enable and disable keyboard and MIDI mapping from your Max patcher, as well as some other useful utilities.

match.name: match
match.digest: Watch for a message match, then output the message
match.description: Watches an incoming stream of ints, floats, symbols, lists, or messages, and outputs the stream after it has met the specification of its arguments.

matrix.name: matrix
matrix.description: The matrix object controls the connections between inlets and outlets. You can route any inlet to any combination of outlets. Each connection has an associated gain factor and all values travelling through matrix can be scaled. It shares the same control protocol with the signal matrix~ object, but unlike matrix~, the outlets of the matrix object do not add the values of multiple inputs. matrix is best understood as a combination of gate and switch with many more features.

matrixctrl.name: matrixctrl
matrixctrl.description: Provides a user interface control containing a group of cells in a grid. Cell states can either be on/off or incremental steps. This object is especially useful for controlling the matrix~ object.

inlets.digest: bang Outputs All Cells, list Sets Cells

max.name: max
max.description: This is where you send those messages

maximum.name: maximum
maximum.description: Watches an input stream for any numbers which are greater than its most recently set maximum. If the input value is less than or equal to the maximum, the maximum value is output. If the input value is greater, that value is output.

outlets.digest: Maximum of Left and Right Inlets

maxurl.name: maxurl
maxurl.description:
maxurl is a wrapper around libcurl that can perform HTTP requests. Use it to fetch and post web content.	For more information on curl please refer to the
		
			Curl Docs Page.
		
		To learn about and practice HTTP Requests, check out
		
			httpbin.org.

inlets.digest: Start an HTTP request (dictionary or message)

mean.name: mean
mean.description: Calculates the mean (average) of all the numbers it has received and outputs it.

menubar.name: menubar
menubar.digest: Put up a custom menu bar
menubar.description: The menubar object provides control over the menu bar. It allows your patch to put up its own menus, and add items to standard File and Edit menus. When a menu item is chosen, the item number is sent out the outlet corresponding to the menu containing the item.

message.name: message
message.description: message displays and sends any given message with the capability to handle specified arguments.

inlets.digest: Trigger the Message, set Changes It

arguments.name: comma

messageview.name: messageview
messageview.description: Use the messageview object to view a stream of messages.

metro.name: metro
metro.digest: Output a bang message at regular intervals
metro.description: Acts as a metronome which outputs bang s at a regular, specified interval. This object uses the Max time format syntax, so the interval that the metro object uses can be either fixed or tempo-relative.

midiflush.name: midiflush
midiflush.digest: Send MIDI note-offs for hanging note-ons
midiflush.description: Analyzes a raw MIDI stream (from midiin or seq), counting the number of note-ons received for each note and MIDI channel. When it is sent a bang, MIDI note-off messages are sent for any notes which have not been turned off.

inlets.digest: Raw MIDI Data, bang flushes held notes

midiformat.name: midiformat
midiformat.digest: Prepare data in the form of a MIDI message
midiformat.description: Numbers received in the inlets are used as data for MIDI messages. The data is formatted into a complete MIDI message (with the status byte determined by the inlet) and sent out the outlet as individual bytes.

midiin.name: midiin
midiin.description: Listens to a specified MIDI port and output the raw MIDI data received.

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

midiinfo.name: midiinfo
midiinfo.digest: Fill a pop-up menu with MIDI device names
midiinfo.description: Outputs a series of messages which will set up a pop-up menu to a list of MIDI output devices when a bang is received. A number in midiinfo's right inlet creates a list of MIDI input devices.

inlets.digest: bang, int List Outputs, int Sets Menu Item
bang, int List Inputs, int Sets Menu Item

midiout.name: midiout
midiout.description: Transmits raw MIDI data to a specified port.

midiparse.name: midiparse
midiparse.description: Separates raw MIDI bytes into standard message types. This object works particularly well formatting the output of the midiin and seq objects.

midiselect.name: midiselect
midiselect.digest: Select and interpret raw MIDI data
midiselect.description: midiselect receives raw MIDI bytes and separates the input stream. MIDI data to be selected for output is set using object attributes. There is a separate data outlet associated with each MIDI data selection attribute. Any input data which is unselected will be sent out the object's eighth outlet.

minimum.name: minimum
minimum.description: Watches an input stream for any numbers which are less than its most recently set minimum. If the input value is greater than or equal to the minimum, the minimum value is output. If the input value is less, that value is output.

outlets.digest: Minimum of Left and Right Inlets

mira.frame.name: mira.frame
mira.frame.digest: Mirror your Max patcher on your iPad
mira.frame.description: Mira.frame defines a rectangle in the Max patcher that will be mirrored on connected iPad devices. Any Mira-supported objects that fall within the rectangle will be visible on the iPad. Multiple mira.frame objects will be rendered on the iPad as separate tabs. When a mira.frame object exists in a patcher, all Mira-supported objects in the patcher are automatically assigned a scripting name.

modifiers.name: modifiers
modifiers.description: Polls and reports the state of the keyboard's modifier keys.

1 if Caps Lock Down, 0 if Up
1 if Option/Alt Key Down, 0 if Up
1 if Ctrl Key Down, 0 if Up
1 if Command/Fn Key Down, 0 if Up

mousestate.name: mousestate

outlets.digest: 1 if Middle Button Down, 0 if Up
1 if Right Button Down, 0 if Up

mpeformat.name: mpeformat
mpeformat.digest: Prepare data in the form of a Multidimensional Polyphonic Expression (MPE) MIDI message
mpeformat.description: Numbers received in the inlets from midiformat objects are routed as MPE-compatible MIDI messages. In addition, the object outputs mpeevent messages for use with instruments hosted by the vst~ object.

mtof.name: mtof
mtof.digest: Convert a MIDI note number to frequency
mtof.description: Performs MIDI-note-number to frequency conversion. Frequency is reported in as a float in Hertz (Hz).

mtr.name: mtr
mtr.description: Records messages and provides sequenced playback.

multislider.name: multislider

multislider.description: Displays data as either an array of sliders or a scrolling display. When configured as sliders, the values are set (and output) as numeric lists. When configured as a scrolling display, multislider receives numbers, plots them, and scrolls the display area.

inlets.digest: list of 2 (0.-1.) floats gives x,y for interpolation

outlets.digest: Interpolated weights for each active node

notein.description: Receives its input from a MIDI note-on or note-off message sent by a MIDI input device.

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

noteout.name: noteout
noteout.description: Transmits note-on and note-off messages to a MIDI device.

nrpnin.name: nrpnin
nrpnin.description: Output the value from a specific Non-Registered Parameter Number (NRPN) and MIDI channel.

nrpnout.name: nrpnout
nrpnout.description: Format 14-bit MIDI Non-Registered Parameter Number messages to be transmitted using the midiout object.

inlets.digest: Parameter Value (0-16383 or list MSB LSB)

nslider.name: nslider
nslider.digest: Output numbers from a notation display
nslider.description: nslider or "Note Slider" is a musical-notation-based integer value slider.

number.name: number
number.digest: Display and output numbers, lists, and messages
number.description: The number object displays and outputs either integers, floats, lists of numbers, or any message, depending on its format. flonum is a synonym for number in Float format, listbox is a synonym for number in List format, and textbox is a synonym for number in Text format.

inlets.digest: Set Displayed Input and Repeat to Output

numkey.name: numkey
numkey.digest: Interpret numbers typed on the keyboard

outlets.digest: Value of Number Typed When Entered
Value of Number While It's Being Typed

offer.name: offer
offer.description: Store two ints as an x, y pair, and access them by x value. When a pair is retrieved, it is deleted from the collection.

inlets.digest: X After Y Stores, X Alone Outputs Y, Deletes

outlets.digest: Y When Corresponding X Is Input

onebang.name: onebang
onebang.description: Allows a bang in the left inlet to pass through ONLY if a bang has been received in the right inlet. After that, a bang in the left inlet will not get through again until a bang has been received again in the right inlet.

inlets.digest: (bang/anything) Set to let one bang through

onecopy.name: onecopy
onecopy.digest: Prevent multiple copies of the same patcher from being opened
onecopy.description: Use the onecopy object inside a patcher that you want to place in the extras folder for inclusion in the Extras menu. When the patcher's name is chosen using the Extras menu, its window will be brought to the front instead of opened a second time if it has already been loaded. The patch will be loaded if it is not currently open. The onecopy object cooperates with the Extras menu to ensure that only one copy of the patcher is opened at a time. However, opening the patcher containing a onecopy object by choosing Open... from the File menu will open additional copies.

opendialog.name: opendialog
opendialog.digest: Open a dialog to ask for a file or folder
opendialog.description: Use the opendialog object to select a file of a specific type or folder from a standard dialog window. To choose a folder, use the "fold" type. opendialog reports the entire pathname of the file or folder chosen, which can be passed to any Max object after the word read or load.

inlets.digest: bang Shows Dialog, symbol(s) Set File Types

arguments.name: file-types
arguments.digest: Set the list of file types

osc.codebox.name: osc.codebox
osc.codebox.description: The osc.codebox object is a UI object for the display of OSC packets as dictionaries.

inlets.digest: OSC packet to display in dictionary format

osc.packet.name: osc.packet
osc.packet.digest: Store and recall an OSC packet
osc.packet.description: The osc.packet object stores and recalls OSC packets, and can convert them into different formats.

inlets.digest: OSC packet to store and output immediately

outlet.name: outlet
outlet.digest: Send messages out of a patcher
outlet.description: Show up as an outlet at the bottom of an object box when the patcher is used inside another patcher (as an object or a subpatch). Messages received by the outlet object will come out of the corresponding outlet in the subpatch's object box.

pack.name: pack
pack.description: Combine items into an output list. The arguments determine the list format and types of the list elements. The number of inlets is based on the number of arguments.

inlets.digest: value for the first list element, causes output
value for the second list element

io_rule.description: Number of arguments determines number of inlets. Default: 2 inlets (0, 0).

pak.name: pak
pak.digest: Output a list when any element changes
pak.description: The pak object (pronounced "pock") offers much of the functionality of pack, but outputs the entire list whenever input is received in any inlet.

inlets.digest: int to be Element 1 in the list
int to be Element 2 in the list

panel.name: panel
panel.description: The panel object lets you create colored panels for use in creating user interfaces. The panel can be a variety of shapes, including circles, triangles, arrows, and rectangles with optional rounded corners. Shadows and gradients can also be used.

param.name: param
param.description:
The param object appears as an attribute on the poly~ object, in addition to being published to the Parameter system, and can be attached to UI objects via the Parameter Connect feature. It currently only supports single numeric values (int/float), but not symbols nor lists.
		
		Parameters defined with the param object will only appear once in the Parameter Window for the top-level patcher containing the hosting poly~ object, regardless of how many voices of polyphony are configured, and changes will be propagated to all voices. This feature is useful for defining "monophonic" parameters which apply to every voice of a polyphonic structure (such as amplitude envelope attack).

param.osc.name: param.osc
param.osc.digest: Control and report info about parameters using OSC.
param.osc.description: Controls and reports info about parameters using OpenSoundControl (OSC).

past.name: past
past.digest: Notify when a threshold is passed
past.description: Sends a bang when a number rises above a certain specified value.

outlets.digest: bang When Numbers Rise Above Specified Value

patcher.name: patcher
patcher.digest: Create a subpatch within a patch

patcherargs.name: patcherargs
patcherargs.description: Retrieves patcher arguments and parses attribute style arguments. "normal" arguments are sent out the left outlet (first), and attribute style arguments are sent out the right outlet (second).

pattr.name: pattr
pattr.digest: Provide an alias with a named data wrapper
pattr.description: Stores its own data, or binds to another object to share its contents with other pattr-based objects (such as pattrstorage). Can be used for data routing or preset creation.

pattrforward.name: pattrforward
pattrforward.digest: Send any message to a named object
pattrforward.description: Routes messages or selects new message routing destinations according to the messages it receives. You can also use the pattrforward object to route messages directly to a specific inlet of an object exposed by pattr or autopattr objects, and also send messages directly to a subpatcher, abstraction or bpatcher.

pattrhub.name: pattrhub
pattrhub.digest: Access all pattr objects in a patcher
pattrhub.description: Centralizes communication with all pattr objects in a patcher.

pattrmarker.name: pattrmarker
pattrmarker.description: The pattrmarker object associates a patcher with a global name, which can be used when looking up named objects. This permits, among other conveniences, name lookup and communication between two or more independent patcher hierarchies.

pattrstorage.name: pattrstorage
pattrstorage.description: View and modify client object data, and store or recall presets.

pcontrol.name: pcontrol
pcontrol.description: Serves as a remote control for patcher/subpatcher windows and functions.

inlets.digest: Control Message to Send to Patcher Window

outlets.digest: Connect to Inlet of Patcher to be Controlled

peak.name: peak
peak.description: Compares a number to a previous peak-value and, if larger, it is sent out the output while the new peak-value is set to that number.

outlets.digest: Goes high for a new peak
Goes high for a number that isn't a new peak

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

pictctrl.name: pictctrl
pictctrl.description: Creating buttons, switches, knobs, and other controls using images from a picture file for its appearance.

inlets.digest: int (0-1) Sets Value, read Opens File

pictslider.name: pictslider
pictslider.description: A slider control that uses pictures in external files for its appearance. It uses two pictures--one for the "knob" and one for the background over which the knob moves. The pictslider object has default pictures that are used if you do not want to supply pictures of your own, but its intended use is creating controls with customized appearances.

inlets.digest: int: Horizontal Value; list: Both Values

pipe.name: pipe
pipe.description: Delays numbers, lists of numbers or symbols. pipe uses the Max time format syntax, so the delay interval can be either fixed or tempo-relative.

arguments.name: initialization
arguments.digest: Initialize values, inlet/outlet count, and delay time

playbar.name: playbar
playbar.digest: Control video or audio file playback
playbar.description: Interacts with movie, imovie, sfplay~ or jit.movie to control the playback, position and looping of the media

inlets.digest: Commands For Controller and Its Movie

outlets.digest: Connect to Movie to be Controlled

poltocar.name: poltocar
poltocar.description: Converts a polar-coordinate pair consisting of distance and angle values into a cartesian-coordinate pair consisting of real and imaginary values.

poly.name: poly
poly.description: Provides polyphonic voice-allocation by allocating data to different individual voices.

inlets.digest: Pitch or list with Pitch and Velocity
port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

polymidiin.name: polymidiin
polymidiin.digest: Output Multidimensional Polyphonic Expression (MPE) MIDI data
polymidiin.description: The polymidiin object is used inside a poly~ instance. It outputs MPE MIDI data.

pong.name: pong
pong.description: Use the pong object to clip, fold, or wrap its input within the range of a low value and a high value.

pow.name: pow
pow.digest: Computes input to the nth power
pow.description: Raises the base value (set in the left inlet) to the power of the exponent (set in the right inlet).

prepend.name: prepend
prepend.digest: Add a message in front of input
prepend.description: Prepends any input messages with a stored message.

inlets.digest: Message to be Followed by Arguments

preset.name: preset
preset.description: preset can store and recall the parameters of any specified user interface objects at the click of a mouse. It can also be used as a user interface link to a named pattrstorage object.

outlets.digest: Connect to Objects to Include in a Preset
Connect to Objects to Exclude From a Preset
Connect to Object to Capture Attributes

print.name: print

print.description: print will print any input into the Max Console for debugging, messaging, or analysis purposes. Selecting "Show Output" from the Object Action Menu opens the sidebar Max console and filters output to only show posts from the print object.

inlets.digest: Anything to be Printed in Max Console

prob.name: prob

inlets.digest: bang Chooses State, list Assigns Transition

pv.name: pv
pv.digest: Share data within a patch hierarchy
pv.description: Creates a value that is shared between objects, but only within a single patcher. Unlike the value object, pv objects that share the same name only share the same value if they are in the same patcher, or one of its subpatches.

inlets.digest: bang Outputs, Anything Else Sets Value

outlets.digest: Value Shared by All Local pv a Objects

pvar.name: pvar
pvar.digest: Connect to a named object in a patcher
pvar.description: The pvar object lets you build user interfaces in one part of your patcher that are associated with the "process" part in another part of the patcher. Unlike the send and receive objects, pvar does not work globally; the pvar object and its associated object must be in the same patcher. To set an object's name, select the object, open the inspector, then enter a name under Name > Scripting Name. The name cannot be a number, although it can contain numbers.

qlim.name: qlim
qlim.description: Slows down throughput of messages. Limiting is based on a minimum amount of time between passed message.

inlets.digest: Sets Output Interval to Send Input

qlist.name: qlist
qlist.description: Stores a collection of timed or untimed "cues" in the form of messages which can be sent either out its outlet or remotely to various receive objects in your patch.

outlets.digest: bang on End of Cue List

qmetro.name: qmetro
qmetro.description: Outputs bang messages at a set interval. Similar to metro, qmetro will drop output if the processing queue is overloaded.

quickthresh.name: quickthresh
quickthresh.description: Combines numbers when they are received close together. quickthresh is a faster, low-latency alternative to thresh that is optimized for chord detection.

inlets.digest: Numbers to be Gathered Into list

radiogroup.name: radiogroup
radiogroup.description: Provides a user interface for option selection. There are two modes of operation: radio button and checkbox.

random.name: random
random.description: Outputs random numbers within the range between 0 and 1 less than the argument specified.

receive.name: receive
receive.description: Receives and outputs messages from send objects sharing the same name. This allows you to send any kind of message between Patcher windows or within a window without using patch cords. A receive object can be instantiated with its short-form: the letter "r".

inlets.digest: set Changes Receive Source (currently not assigned)

regexp.name: regexp
regexp.digest: Use regular expressions to process input
regexp.description: With regexp, it's possible to use PERL-compatible regular expressions (PCRE) to match or make substitutions within symbols and lists.

relativepath.name: relativepath
relativepath.digest: Convert an absolute to a relative path
relativepath.description: Converts an absolute path to one that is relative to the Max application folder.

outlets.digest: Path Relative to Max Application Folder

repl.name: repl
repl.digest: Send, receive and output Max Console commands
repl.description:
The repl object creates a named destination ("target") for commands you type into the Max Console window, creating a way to control patchers with a command line interface. (REPL is an acronym for Read Evaluate Print Loop.) Targets are either global -- receiving commands from any Max Console window -- or local to the patcher for which a Max Console is in the right sidebar. You can send a response to a command back to the user by sending any message to the inlet of repl.		
		
		You can also use repl as a way to evalate commands as if they were typed into the Max Console window as well as print to the console.

inlets.digest: Evaluate and/or Print Messages to the Max Console

round.name: round
round.description: Calculates and outputs an integer multiple of any given number.

route.digest: Select outlet based on input matching
route.description: Tries to match a message's first argument to the route object's own arguments. The rightmost outlet passes any message that matched no other choice, so you may gang route objects to get more choices

inlets.digest: Number or list to be Routed
Number or list to be Routed

io_rule.description: Outlets = arg count + 1 (extra outlet for unmatched). Default: 3 outlets.

routepass.name: routepass
routepass.digest: Route a complete incoming message based on input matching
routepass.description: Tries to match a message's first argument to the route object's own arguments. Unlike the route object, routepass does not strip off the matched portion of the message. The rightmost outlet passes any message that matched no other choice, so you may gang route objects to get more choices

inlets.digest: Number or list to be Routed

io_rule.description: Outlets = arg count + 1 (extra outlet passes unmatched). Default: 3 outlets.

inlets.digest: connect, disconnect, patch, clear, dump, print

io_rule.description: First arg = inlets, second arg = outlets. Variable routing matrix.

rpnin.name: rpnin
rpnin.description: Output the value from a specific Registered Parameter Number (RPN) and MIDI channel.

inlets.digest: Parameter Value (0-16383 or list MSB LSB)

rslider.digest: Display or change a range of numbers

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

savedialog.description: Opens a file dialog for selection or entry of a filename to write. savedialog takes a list of one or more file types and allows you to save a file and choose a type for it.

inlets.digest: bang Shows Dialog, symbol(s) Set File Types

arguments.name: filetypes
arguments.digest: Sets one or more file types that will be displayed as choices for the user.

scale.name: scale
scale.digest: Map values to an output range
scale.description: The scale object maps an input range of float or integer values to an output range. The output range can be larger or smaller than the input range, can be inverted and can change numeric type. If specified, the mapping can also be exponential. Please note that unanticipated results can occur when using integer arguments, as output values are truncated rather than rounded. For precise output values, use float arguments.

arguments.name: input-low
arguments.digest: Set the low end of the input range

arguments.name: input-high
arguments.digest: Set the high end of the input range

arguments.name: output-low
arguments.digest: Set the low end of the output range

arguments.name: output-high
arguments.digest: Set the high end of the output range

arguments.name: exponential
arguments.digest: Determine the curve of value mapping

schedule.name: schedule
schedule.digest: Schedule messages on high priority scheduler thread
schedule.description: The schedule object schedules messages to execute on the high priority scheduler thread, optionally with a delay time (ms), and optionally deferring to main application thread after the delay time. Similar to the delay and pipe objects, but supports arbitrary messages.

arguments.name: delay
arguments.digest: Set the delay time in ms

screensize.name: screensize
screensize.description: Reports the resolution to which the computer's monitor is currently set.

select.name: select
select.digest: Output bangs based on input matching
select.description: Selectively outputs a bang in response to any input which matches its arguments and will output non-matching messages out its right-most outlet.

io_rule.description: Outlets = arg count + 1 (extra outlet for non-match). Default: 2 outlets.

send.name: send
send.description: send will transmit given messages to receive objects which are named by the same argument and will allow you to send any kind of message between Patcher windows or within a window without using patch cords. A send object can be instantiated simply by typing into an object box the short-form letter "s".

inlets.digest: Message to Send to receive a

seq.name: seq
seq.digest: Sequencer for recording and playing MIDI data
seq.description: seq is a sequencer of raw MIDI bytes. You can control the speed of playback (only at the time you start it), read and write from files, and record from live MIDI input. See also midiparse and midiformat which you may need to get to and from raw MIDI.

inlets.digest: MIDI Data In, Many Other Messages

serial.name: serial
serial.digest: Send and receive from a serial port

inlets.digest: int, list Send Chars, bang Polls Output

setclock.name: setclock
setclock.digest: Create and control an alternative clock
setclock.description: Allows the creation of alternatives to the standard millisecond clock. This includes several modes of timing that change timing activities. Each setclock is associated with a name (its first argument), and this name may be passed as the argument to a "clock" message to numerous objects that use timing in Max, such as metro, line, and pipe.

sin.name: sin
sin.description: Use the sin object to calculate and output the sine of any given number.

arguments.name: initial-value
arguments.digest: Sets the initial value for the sine function

sinh.name: sinh
sinh.description: Use the sinh object to calculate and output the hyperbolic sine of any given number.

arguments.name: initial-value
arguments.digest: Sets the initial value for the hyperbolic sine function

slide.name: slide
slide.description: Filters an input value logarithmically between changes. It's particularly useful for envelope following and lowpass filtering to smooth a stream of continuous data.

slider.name: slider
slider.digest: Move a slider to output values
slider.description: Resembles a sliding potentiometer, outputting numbers restricted to a specified range, offset by a specified number, and multiplied by a specified number.

outlets.digest: Outputs Value When Slider is Changed

speedlim.name: speedlim

inlets.digest: Sets Output Interval to Send Input

spell.name: spell
spell.description: Accepts a numeric stream and outputs and outputs ASCII characters. Options arguments provide for forced-length messages.

split.name: split
split.digest: Look for a range of numbers
split.description: Check if input can fall within a specified range. If it falls within that range, it sends the number out the left outlet and if it does not, then it sends the number out the right outlet.

inlets.digest: Value to be Sent to One of the Two Outlets
Set Lower Limit for Left Outlet
Set Upper Limit for Left Outlet

spray.name: spray

spray.description: Accepts lists as input, where the first number is taken as the outlet number, and one or more values that follow are sent out that outlet and those to its right, in right-to-left order. The argument sets the number of outlets (there is no set limit). The default number of outlets is 2.

inlets.digest: list: Outlet Number Followed by Data

io_rule.description: First arg sets outlet count. Inlets = 1. Default: 2 outlets.

sprintf.name: sprintf
sprintf.digest: Format a message of words and numbers
sprintf.description: Uses the common C-language "printf" function inside Max. You can combine symbols, organize lists of numbers, or format messages or menu items. For complete documentation, refer to a standard C library reference manual.

sqrt.name: sqrt
sqrt.description: Calculates and outputs the square-root of any given number.

standalone.name: standalone
standalone.description: The standalone object lets you set options for creating a standalone application from a Max patch, and is used in conjunction with the Build Application/Collective... item found in the File menu. You should only have one standalone object in your top-level patch.

stepcounter.name: stepcounter
stepcounter.description: The stepcounter object uses messages to advance through a sequence of count values. The output producted by stepcounter can be used to generate a sequence of durations from a metro.

string.name: string

string.trim.name: string.trim
string.trim.digest: Trim whitespace from the beginning and end of a string object

string.trimend.name: string.trimend
string.trimend.digest: Trim whitespace from the end of a string object
string.trimend.description: Whitespace at the beginning of the string is ignored. Whitespace includes space, tab and newline characters.

string.trimstart.name: string.trimstart
string.trimstart.digest: Remove whitespace from the beginning of a string object
string.trimstart.description: Whitespace includes spaces, newlines, tabs, and carriage returns.

string.utf8.name: string.utf8
string.utf8.digest: Iterate the UTF-8 characters in a string as integers
string.utf8.description: Multibyte characters are output as multibyte (> 127) integers.

string.withpass.name: string.withpass
string.withpass.digest: Route a string by a matching substring
string.withpass.description: Route a string to one of several outputs based on a substring match. Similar to routepass but for strings. This object was renamed from string.passcmp (which is now an alias to this object), the functionality remains the same.

outlets.digest: string out if there are no matches

stripnote.name: stripnote
stripnote.description: Only pass note-on messages: those having any velocity above 0.

strippath.name: strippath
strippath.digest: Separate filename from a full pathname
strippath.description: Removes path information from a complete file path. strippath also tells you whether the resulting filename is in the search path or not.

outlets.digest: 1 if Path/File in Search Path, 0 Otherwise

substitute.name: substitute
substitute.description: Matches messages to its own arguments; whenever it finds a match, will make the appropriate substitution.

suckah.name: suckah
suckah.digest: Get a pixel from the display
suckah.description: Creates a click-able area that returns an RGB color, or can accept any screen coordinates to get the RGB values of that pixel.

suspend.name: suspend
suspend.digest: Reports when the application is in the background or foreground
suspend.description: Outputs a 0 when the application is in the background, and a 1 when the application is in the foreground.

inlets.digest: 1 When Application Is In Foreground, 0 When In Background

outlets.digest: 1 When Application Is In Foreground, 0 When In Background

sustain.name: sustain
sustain.description: Holds any notes with velocities of 0 until instructed to release them. This provides for sustain pedal-like behavior when working with incoming MIDI notes.

swap.name: swap
swap.description: Swaps the values of its inlets, preserving right-to-left ordering. The first outlet type is determined by its argument. The second outlet's type is always an int.

inlets.digest: Sets Value for Right Outlet, Causes Output

swatch.name: swatch
swatch.description: Provides 2-dimensional selection and display of colors, representing hue along the horizontal axis and lightness along the vertical axis. A third color dimension, saturation, may be set by means of the saturation message.

switch.name: switch
switch.digest: Accept messages from a specific inlet

inlets.digest: 0 Turns switch Off, Non-zero Selects Input

outlets.digest: Output if Open Input Receives Anything

io_rule.description: First arg sets inlet count (+1 for control). Outlets = 1. Default: 2 inlets.

sxformat.name: sxformat
sxformat.description: Accepts, as arguments, a list of numbers which are sent out sequentially. In addition, you may use one or more "expr"-style statements, which start with the word "is", which will be evaluated with the result being sent out. "is" statements need to be separated by slashes.

sysexin.name: sysexin
sysexin.description: Receives MIDI system exclusive messages from a MIDI input device. It takes an optional argument for port selection. If a MIDI system exclusive message is received, sysexin outputs raw MIDI as integers.

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

tab.name: tab
tab.description: The tab can be used to create multiple button and multiple column displays and interfaces.

tabcontroller.name: tabcontroller
tabcontroller.description: The tabcontroller object is placed in a top-level patcher. When provided with a dictionary of tab names and patcher references, it will create tabs in that patcher for each dictionary entry. References in the dictionary can refer to embedded subpatchers or patcher files. When patcher files are used, they will not be loaded until you click on the tab. Moreoever, tabs can be shown in a vertically scrolling list at the left side of the patcher window. These features permit a patcher using tabcontroller to show an arbitrarily large list of patchers, making it ideal for presenting a set of related tutorials or examples. Note that RNBO or Gen subpatchers in tabs are loaded immediately when loading their parent patchers.

table.name: table
table.digest: Store and edit an array of numbers

inlets.digest: int Outputs Value at Index, list Stores Value
Sets Y Value for Next int at Left Inlet

outlets.digest: Outputs bang When Table Window Edited

tan.name: tan
tan.description: Use the tan object to calculate and output the tangent of any given number.

arguments.name: initial-value
arguments.digest: Sets the initial value for the tangent function

tanh.name: tanh
tanh.description: Use the tanh object to calculate and output the hyperbolic tangent of any given number.

arguments.name: initial-value
arguments.digest: Sets the initial value for the hyperbolic tangent function

tempo.name: tempo
tempo.digest: Output numbers at a metronomic tempo
tempo.description: Produces metronomic output controllable in beats per minute, and with specifiable whole-note divisions.

inlets.digest: Beat Divisions of a Whole Note

text.name: text
text.digest: Format messages as a text file
text.description: Collects and formats incoming messages as text to be output as lines of text.

inlets.digest: Incoming Numbers or Symbols to be Collected

text.codebox.name: text.codebox
text.codebox.description: The dict.codebox object is a UI object for display and editing of text, optionally backed by a file on disk.  Clicking on the hammer icon will output the text as a string and optionally write the file back to disk (autowrite must be enabled). Beware of losing work when enabling autowrite. It can use a syntax highlighting style (.js, .json, ,jxs, .coll, .genexpr, .lua, or .cpp)

textbox.name: textbox
textbox.digest: Display and output numbers, lists, and messages
textbox.description: The number object displays and outputs either integers, floats, lists of numbers, or any message, depending on its format. flonum is a synonym for number in Float format, listbox is a synonym for number in List format, and textbox is a synonym for number in Text format.

inlets.digest: Set Displayed Input and Repeat to Output

textedit.name: textedit
textedit.description: Provides a user interface within a patcher which can collect typed-in text from the computer-keyboard.

outlets.digest: Sends "textchanged" when Text is Changed While Typing

themecolor.name: themecolor
themecolor.digest: Change and/or listen for changes in interface colors
themecolor.description: Output the interface color whenever it changes, query Max for current color settings, and change an interface color.

inlets.digest: list Sets Color, bang Reports Color

arguments.name: UI element name
arguments.digest: name of the current UI element

thisobject.name: thisobject
thisobject.description: Use thisobject as a connection to objects by name. thisobject can be used with global named objects including max and dsp. It can also connect to objects with scripting names, including objects in other patchers using pattr paths. You can also use the name patcher for connecting to the patcher containing the thisobject, which works similarly to the thispatcher object. thisobject is similar to getattr but permits you to change the named target object using the target attribute, whereas getattr uses a patch cord to establish a connection to its target.

inlets.digest: Control Inlet, bang Reports Attribute Value

thispatcher.name: thispatcher
thispatcher.description: Allows modification of a patcher window with Max messages.

outlets.digest: Pathname If patcher Is a File

threadcheck.name: threadcheck
threadcheck.digest: Report the thread of execution for a message
threadcheck.description: Report the thread of execution for a message

thresh.name: thresh
thresh.digest: Combine numbers, symbols and lists when received close together
thresh.description: Collects items into a list if they appear within a certain specifiable amount of time. Each time an item arrives, the time is reset.

inlets.digest: Numbers to be Gathered Into list

timepoint.name: timepoint
timepoint.description: Outputs a bang when the clock reaches a specific time. The clock has to be moving forward in order for the bang to be output.

outlets.digest: bang When Clock Reaches Target Time

timer.name: timer
timer.digest: Report elapsed time between two events
timer.description: timer starts keeping time when a bang is sent to the left inlet. The right outlet can report elapsed time in milliseconds or one of the Max time format options, with the exception of notevalues.

inlets.digest: Start of Interval to be Timed
End of Interval to be Timed

outlets.digest: Milliseconds Between bangs at Left and Right Inlets
Formatted Value of Time Between bangs at Left and Right Inlets

togedge.name: togedge
togedge.description: Output bangs for 0-to-1 transitions out the left outlet, and bangs the right outlet for 1-to-0 transitions. Outlets alternate output when bangs are received.

inlets.digest: Connect to a checkbox (or bang it to toggle outs)

toggle.name: toggle
toggle.digest: Switch between off and on (0 and 1)
toggle.description: When clicked, toggle outputs a 0 when turned off and a 1 when turned on. When giving input, a non-zero number will turn it on, a 0 will turn it off, and a bang will alternate the state of the toggle. All numbers are converted to integer and passed through unchanged.

inlets.digest: int Sets Toggle, bang Reverses It

outlets.digest: Output 1 or 0 When Toggle Is Set

tosymbol.name: tosymbol
tosymbol.digest: Convert messages, numbers, or lists to a single symbol
tosymbol.description: tosymbol accepts any message, number, or list, and converts it into a single symbol. The symbol has a maximum length of 2048 characters.

touchin.name: touchin
touchin.description: Outputs from MIDI aftertouch (channel pressure) messages received from a MIDI input device.

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

touchout.name: touchout
touchout.description: Transmits MIDI aftertouch values to a MIDI device.

translate.name: translate
translate.description: The translate object converts from any of the fixed or relative Max time values to any other fixed or relative time value.

transport.name: transport
transport.description: Starts and stops the passage of time for objects linked to a transport. If given a name, the transport object will control a time context of the given name, otherwise it will control Max's global 'internal' transport. The transport object reports time consistent with the time formats used in Max.

trigger.name: trigger
trigger.description: Outputs any input received in order from right to left and formatted according to the object-argument specified.

inlets.digest: Message to be Fanned to Multiple Outputs

io_rule.description: Number of arguments determines number of outlets. Each argument (i, f, b, l, s, or a constant) creates one outlet. Default: 2 outlets (int, int).

trough.name: trough
trough.digest: Output a number if it is less than previous numbers
trough.description: Compares an input value to the stored value and, if it's smaller it sends the input to the output and sets it as the new trough.

outlets.digest: Goes high for a new trough
Goes high for a number that isn't a new trough

ubutton.name: ubutton
ubutton.description: Creates a transparant click-able region that can be placed over graphics or other objects. Produces a bang message when clicked.

inlets.digest: Inverts Button and Sends bang out Left Outlet

outlets.digest: bang on Mouse-Up or Incoming Message
1 if Mouse Clicked Inside Button, 0 if Outside

udpreceive.name: udpreceive
udpreceive.description: Receives messages transmitted over a network using the User Datagram Protocol (UDP).

udpsend.name: udpsend
udpsend.description: Transmits messages over a network using the User Datagram Protocol (UDP).

umenu.name: umenu
umenu.description: Displays text as a pop-up menu. Selections can be made manually, or set incoming numbers. Outputs both selection number and selection text.

outlets.digest: Menu Item Text Evaluated as a Message

universal.name: universal
universal.digest: Send messages to all objects of the same type
universal.description: Sends a input message to all instances of the same class of object in a patcher. The order in which the messages arrive is not defined.

unjoin.name: unjoin
unjoin.description: Separates a list's elements by group, and send each group of items out a separate outlet.

io_rule.description: Number of arguments determines number of outlets. Default: 2 outlets.

unpack.name: unpack
unpack.digest: Break a list into individual messages
unpack.description: Breaks a list into its elements, and sends each item out a separate outlet.

io_rule.description: Number of arguments determines number of outlets. Default: 2 outlets.

urn.name: urn
urn.description: Outputs random numbers and keeps track of each number that has been generated. When all numbers up to the maximum (set via an argument or the right inlet) have been output, a bang is send out the right outlet.

outlets.digest: bang If All Numbers in Range Chosen

uzi.name: uzi
uzi.description: Outputs a specified number of bang messages quickly. uzi is designed for rapid-fire output of a large number of bang messages.

v8.name: v8
v8.description: Exposes the Javascript language (ECMAScript 6+) and some Max specific extensions. The v8 object can be instantiated with a javascript filename or with numerical arguments to specify the number of outlets and inlets respectively. The default number of outlets and inlets are both 1.

v8.codebox.name: v8.codebox
v8.codebox.description: Exposes the Javascript language (ECMAScript 6+) and some Max specific extensions, using a UI object for display and editing.

v8ui.name: v8ui
v8ui.digest: Javascript user interfaces and graphics (Modern Engine)
v8ui.description: Provides an environment to make user interface elements using Javascript (ECMAScript 6+). This provides all of the programming tools available in the v8 object, but also exposes the mgraphics and sketch drawing routines for visual output.

value.name: value
value.digest: Share data between other value objects
value.description: Shares data with other value objects of the same name. If you send a message (number, list, or anything else) to one value object, it is set for all value objects with the same 'name' argument. You can get the contents out of a particular value object by sending it a bang.

inlets.digest: int or float Sets Value, bang Causes Output

outlets.digest: Value Shared by All value a Objects

vdp.name: vdp
vdp.digest: Control a videodisk player through a serial port
vdp.description:
The vdp object works with serially-controlled videodisk players (remember them?) that are compatible with the Pioneer 4200 or 8000 standard. Each command received by the vdp object sends a stream of numbers out the object's left outlet, intended to be connected to the serial object. The description of each command discusses what effect the command has on the player, not the exact character stream sent by vdp.		
		
		Because videodisc players have relatively buffer-less serial interfaces, vdp places each command it receives in a queue, and sends it out only when the player has finished executing its most recent command. This "feature" may cause a delay between the time a command is sent to the vdp object and the time it is actually sent out the serial port.
		
		
		Any message received in the right inlet will behave exactly as if it had been received in the left inlet, except that it will be put at the front of the queue, to be the very next command sent out to the player.

vexpr.name: vexpr
vexpr.digest: Evaluate a math expression for a list
vexpr.description: Performs mathematical calculations using C language-style mathematical operations. Operates on inputs that are lists rather than collections of single values.

arguments.name: format
arguments.digest: The data format for an inlets

vstscan.name: vstscan
vstscan.description: Vstscan provides an interface to audio plugin scanning so that this functionality may be presented in patchers and standalones. You can use vstscan to build menus of plugin names for use in patcher code.

when.name: when
when.description: Outputs the current time of a transport in ticks and bars/beats/units.

xbendin.name: xbendin
xbendin.digest: Interpret extra precision MIDI pitch bend values
xbendin.description: Acts as a 14-bit pitch bend filter/processor and xbendin2 serves as a 2-byte pitch bend filter/processor.

xbendout.name: xbendout
xbendout.digest: Format extra precision MIDI pitch bend messages
xbendout.description: Formats messages which occupy both bytes of the MIDI pitch bend message. xbendout2 allows you to send the pitch bend data as two bytes from 0-127.

xctlin.name: xctlin
xctlin.description: Output the value from a specific 14-bit controller pair and MIDI channel.

xctlout.name: xctlout
xctlout.description: Format 14-bit MIDI continuous controller messages to be transmitted using the midiout object.

inlets.digest: Controller Value (0-16383 or list MSB LSB)

xmidiin.name: xmidiin
xmidiin.description: Listens to a specified MIDI port and output the raw MIDI data received.

inlets.digest: port Message Sets MIDI Input Port/Device

xnotein.name: xnotein
xnotein.digest: Interpret MIDI note messages with release velocity

xnoteout.name: xnoteout
xnoteout.digest: Format MIDI note messages with release velocity

zl.name: zl
zl.description: Performs several kinds of list processing functions. You set the function with an argument, and can change the function performed with the mode message. The behavior of the zl object's inlets and outlets vary according to the selected mode.

outlets.digest: index(es) of the non equal item(s)

zl.delace.name: zl.delace
zl.delace.description: Splits the input list into two lists consisting of every other item. The left outlet will start with the first element, and the right outlet will start with the second element. If the input list is 6.2 3 5.6 5.3 3.8 2.4 the left output list is 6.2 5.6 3.8 and the right output list is 3 5.3 2.4).

zl.ecils.name: zl.ecils
zl.ecils.digest: Slice a list in reverse order
zl.ecils.description: Slices a list in reverse order: output last N out right outlet, the rest out the left. The right outlet is output first.

inlets.digest: list to split into two slices

zl.group.name: zl.group
zl.group.description: Output a list after the number of items specified by the maximum output length are received.

inlets.digest: items to collect into a list

zl.indexmap.name: zl.indexmap
zl.indexmap.digest: Create new list from list of indexes
zl.indexmap.description: Creates a new list based on indices of an incoming list (0-based).

zl.iter.name: zl.iter
zl.iter.digest: Successively output lists of specific size
zl.iter.description: Outputs lists of specific sizes successively. The final list output may be shorter than the number, depending on the stored contents of the object.

zl.join.name: zl.join
zl.join.description: Combines two lists and outputs a single list.

outlets.digest: both segments joined as one list

arguments.name: initial-list
arguments.digest: Set the initial right side list

zl.lace.name: zl.lace
zl.lace.description: Interleaves two lists. If the left input list is 6.2 5.6 3.8 and the right input list is 3 5.3 2.4 the output list is 6.2 3 5.6 5.3 3.8 2.4

arguments.name: initial-list
arguments.digest: Set the initial right inlet list

zl.lookup.name: zl.lookup
zl.lookup.description: Outputs the elements of an input list. Element indexing begins at 0.

inlets.digest: position (start at 0) in list to extract
list from which to extract element

zl.median.name: zl.median
zl.median.digest: Get the median value of a list of numbers

zl.mth.name: zl.mth
zl.mth.description: Extract an item from an incoming list, based on the index specified (zero-based).

inlets.digest: list from which to extract mth element

outlets.digest: source list with mth element removed

arguments.name: index-sub
arguments.digest: Initial index and item to substitute

zl.nth.name: zl.nth
zl.nth.description: Extract an item from an incoming list, based on the index specified (starting at 1).

inlets.digest: list from which to extract nth element

outlets.digest: source list with nth element removed

arguments.name: index-sub
arguments.digest: Initial index and item to substitute

zl.queue.name: zl.queue
zl.queue.digest: Output elements of a list in the order they are received
zl.queue.description: Functions as a first-in-first-out (FIFO) stack. Each bang outputs the oldest message received and removes it from the list.

zl.reg.name: zl.reg
zl.reg.description: zl.reg functions as a register that holds a list. A list received in the left inlet is sent out the left outlet immediately. A list received in the right inlet is stored. A bang sends the stored list out the left outlet.

inlets.digest: list to store and output immediately

zl.rot.name: zl.rot
zl.rot.description: zl.rot is used to rotate the contents of a list. An argument is used to specify the number of places a list item is to be rotated - positive numbers rotate the list to the right, and negative numbers rotate left. This value can also be specified as an int input in the right inlet.

zl.scramble.name: zl.scramble
zl.scramble.description: zl.scramble accepts a list in either inlet. A list in the left inlet will cause the object to immediately output a scrambled version of the input-list (a list containing the same elements as the input-list but in a randomized order). A list in the right inlet will set the input-list without causing output (a subsequent bang received in the left inlet will cause a randomized version of the right-input list to be sent out the object's left outlet). On receiving a bang or list in the left inlet, a list of the reordered indexes will be sent out the objects' right outlet.

zl.sect.name: zl.sect
zl.sect.digest: Find common items between two lists
zl.sect.description: zl.sect accepts a list in both inlets and sends a list out the left outlet that contains the elements that are common to both lists.

outlets.digest: list of items in common between both input lists
bang if nothing is common between both input lists

zl.slice.name: zl.slice
zl.slice.description: zl.slice is used to divide a list into two lists. This mode takes a number argument which specifies the size, in elements, of a list. This value can also be specified as an int to the right inlet. A list received in the left inlet will be split into two lists - the first list contains the number of items specified by the argument, and is sent out the left outlet. Any remaining list elements are sent out the right outlet of the object. Note: Lists are sent out the right outlet first.

inlets.digest: list to split into two slices

zl.sort.name: zl.sort
zl.sort.digest: Arrange a list in alphanumeric order
zl.sort.description: zl.sort is used to sort the contents of a list. An argument is used to specify the sorting order. An int of -1 sorts the input list in descending order, and any other value sorts the input list in ascending order. This value can also be specified as an input in the right inlet.

zl.stack.name: zl.stack
zl.stack.digest: Output elements of a list in reverse order
zl.stack.description: zl.stack functions as a last-in-first-out (LIFO) stack; a bang outputs the most recently received message and removes it from the currently held list.

zl.stream.name: zl.stream
zl.stream.digest: Make a list of a certain size
zl.stream.description: zl.stream accepts a number in the right inlet which specifies the length of the output list. Following the receipt of this number, the object will collect this number of items input through the left inlet. After the list-length is complete, and with each subsequent input, the list will be output the left outlet. A 1 or a 0 will be output from the right outlet depending on whether the list-length has been reached or not. A 1 signifies that the list-length has been reached and that the object is now collecting the stream. Use the zlclear message to reset the list.

zl.sub.name: zl.sub
zl.sub.digest: Output position for each occurance of right list in left
zl.sub.description: zl.sub accepts a list in both inlets and sends the output position (starting at 1) for each occurrence of right list in the left list out the left outlet.

outlets.digest: number of sub-list matches (0 if none)

zl.sum.name: zl.sum
zl.sum.description: Returns the sum of a list of numbers.

zl.swap.name: zl.swap
zl.swap.description: zl.swap accepts a list as an argument. A list of two numbers (0-based) in the right inlet will indicate a pair of indices to swap in the list received in the left inlet. For instance, if 1 3 is received in the right inlet, A B C D received in the left inlet will cause the object to output A D C B from the left output.

zl.thin.name: zl.thin
zl.thin.description: Removes all of the elements of the input list which are duplicates and outputs a new list.

zl.union.name: zl.union
zl.union.digest: Combine two lists without duplicating shared items
zl.union.description: zl.union accepts a list in both inlets and sends a list out the left outlet that contains the contents of both input lists. The output does not duplicate items in the right list that are in the left list.

outlets.digest: all items in both input lists, no duplicates

zl.unique.name: zl.unique
zl.unique.description: zl.unique removes any items found in the left input that are present in the right input and outputs a new list.

outlets.digest: list of items in the left list which are not in the right list

zmap.name: zmap
zmap.digest: Maps input range of values to output range
zmap.description: Maps an input range of values and to an output range of values. Similar to scale, but clips values to the ranges, and does not allow inverted scaling.

|.description: Performs a bit-by-bit OR of two numbers (expressed in binary for the task). Outputs a number composed of all those bits which are 1 in either of the two numbers.

inlets.digest: Set Left Operand, Trigger the Calculation

||.description: Compares one number to another and outputs a 1 if the either number is non-zero or a 0 if both number are 0.

inlets.digest: Set Left Operand, Trigger the Calculation
